description: F9 — Lean 4 mathlib4: Functional Proximity Law on Formal Mathematics

hypotheses.statement: The Functional Proximity Law holds in formal mathematics: import coupling (d1) and proof co-development (d3) are positively correlated across Lean 4 mathlib4 top-level modules — r(import_graph, proof_co_development) > 0 with p < 0.05.
The top hub in import_graph (d1) is Algebra or Analysis (the foundational modules most imported by other areas of mathematics).
Hub identity is preserved across layers: at least 2 of the top-3 import_graph hubs also appear in the top-3 proof_co_development hubs.

notes: F9 — IRDME primitivity hypothesis test. d1 = Lean 4 import statements (declared coupling). d3 = git commit co-change with threshold >= 5 co-changes out of 200-commit window (behavioral coupling). n=20 top-level Mathlib modules. Threshold pre-specified before any correlation computation. Nodes: mathematical modules only; infrastructure (Tactic, Deprecated, Testing, Util, Lean) excluded. Source: leanprover-community/mathlib4, depth=200 commits.
